title: 10 best herbal tinctures for gum infection

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with gum infection because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in the tincture, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections, while also reducing swelling and pain associated with inflammation. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of pot marigold help to promote a healthy mouth environment by preventing the spread of infection and supporting the healing process.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Marigold tincture has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans, which are common causes of gum infections, helping to prevent the spread of infection and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving discomfort and promoting oral health.

Wound-healing property: Marigold tincture contains flavonoids that have been shown to accelerate wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and reducing the risk of infection in open wounds such as those caused by gum infections.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that cause gum infections.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Calendula officinalis have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to gum infections.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Calendula officinalis have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and combat the microorganisms that cause gum infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary source of calendula's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat gum infections.
Leaves: They possess similar properties to the flowers and are often used in combination to enhance the effectiveness of the tincture.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that contribute to the antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects of the tincture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowers from a clean and well-drained area at peak bloom with scissors or pinch off blooms.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 2-3 weeks.
Combine 1 part flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow the mixture to macerate for 2-6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a separate container discard the solids and label the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with gum infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The flavonoids present in chamomile have been shown to reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from the discomfort associated with gum infections. Additionally, chamomile's antibacterial compounds help combat the underlying bacterial cause of the infection, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora and speeding up the healing process. As a natural remedy, chamomile tinctures offer a gentle and effective approach to managing gum infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infection, making it easier to treat and manage the condition.

Antimicrobial: Chamomile's antimicrobial properties can help combat the bacterial infection causing the gum disease by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and promoting a healthy environment in the mouth.
Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gum disease and slow down healing.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with gum infections, making it easier to manage symptoms and prevent further complications.
Furanocoumarins (e.g., bergapten): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to combat the bacterial growth that causes gum infections and promote a healthy oral environment.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it easier to manage gum infections and prevent further damage.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of apigenin and bisabolol, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat gum infections.
Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla contain flavonoids and terpenoids that possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating gum infections.
Roots: The roots of the plant contain sesquiterpenes and flavonoids that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which help in treating gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak potency and dry them thoroughly in a warm dark place for 7 days.
Combine 1 part dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years and use 20 to 30 drops per dose as needed.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid and kaempferol, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, making it easier to manage gum infections.

Antimicrobial activity: The tincture has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties against various bacteria, including those responsible for gum infections like Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis, helping to prevent further infection and promote healing.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower's antioxidant compounds, such as quercetin and luteolin, help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and exacerbate infections, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The Echinacea angustifolia, also known as purple coneflower, contains compounds like cinnamic acid and alkamides that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for gum infections. These bioactive compounds can penetrate deep into the affected area, effectively killing off harmful microorganisms and promoting a healthy oral environment. As a result, purple coneflower tinctures may help reduce inflammation, alleviate pain and discomfort, and even prevent further infection.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids in Echinacea purpurea may help with gum infection by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum disease.

Caffeic acid: Caffeic acid, a phenolic compound, may help with gum infection by displaying antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which can protect against oxidative stress and inhibit the growth of pathogens that contribute to gum disease.

Cichoric acid: Cichoric acid, another phenolic compound, may help with gum infection by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are considered the most medicinal part of the plant, containing the highest concentration of bioactive compounds that can help combat gum infections.

Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections in the gums.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm gum inflammation, reducing the risk of gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material completely to prevent contamination or spoilage.
Combine 1 part of the dried plant material with 2 parts of vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid from the plant material and transfer it to a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with gum infection because it possesses antiseptic properties that combat bacteria responsible for inflammation and damage to the gums. The antimicrobial compounds in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid, inhibit the growth of pathogens, reducing redness, swelling, and pain associated with gum infections. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce tissue damage and promote healing, making it a natural remedy for soothing sore gums and promoting oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tincture's antimicrobial compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help to combat the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in lemon balm tincture possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, allowing for a smoother recovery process.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tincture's antioxidant compounds protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue growth and reducing the risk of further infection.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial growth and inflammation associated with gum infections.
Linalool: A terpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can reduce swelling and prevent the spread of infection in the gums.
Geraniol: A terpene that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate gum inflammation, reduce pain, and prevent further infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and antimicrobial properties, which help combat gum infection-causing bacteria.
Stems: Stems are also used for tincture production, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in gum infection relief.
Roots: Roots of Melissa officinalis contain sesquiterpenes, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities that may help alleviate symptoms of gum infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the melissa leaves and flowers in the vodka for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Discard the solids and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dark place.
Allow the tincture to mature for 4 to 6 weeks before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves tinctures helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The eugenol present in cloves has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis. Additionally, clove tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infection, promoting a healthy oral environment. As a natural remedy, it provides an effective alternative to conventional treatments, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Clove oil's antimicrobial compounds, such as eugenol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, including Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in clove oil, particularly epicatechin, exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which helps reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, making it easier to manage symptoms like redness, bleeding, and sensitivity.

Antioxidant properties: Clove's high antioxidant content helps protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy oral environment that's less susceptible to infection and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Eugenol has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria causing gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, while also exhibiting antimicrobial activity against pathogens.

Gallic acid: As a phenolic compound, gallic acid exhibits strong antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat gum infections by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their antibacterial properties that help combat gum infection-causing bacteria.
Buds: Employed for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and heal gum infections.
Seeds: Utilized for their antimicrobial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Gather syzygium aromaticum  dried flowers in quantities of 1 part to 2 parts of carrier solvent.
Combine syzygium aromaticum dried flowers with 80 proof vodka in a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass container for 2 to 4 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove syzygium aromaticum particles.
Bottle the syzygium aromaticum tincture in dark glass bottles and store in a cool dark place indefinitely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Basil tinctures helps with gum infection because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract contains compounds like eugenol, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for periodontal disease. Additionally, basil's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum infections. By applying basil tincture to the affected area, it helps combat infection and promote healing, ultimately reducing the risk of further complications and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Basil's antibacterial compounds, such as eugenol and citronellol, help combat bacteria that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in basil, like caffeic acid and ros marin, reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, allowing the gums to heal more quickly.

remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat gum infection by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that cause the infection.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help prevent the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation in the gums, thereby treating gum infection.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Ocimum basilicum are rich in antibacterial properties, which help combat gum infection-causing bacteria.
Flowers: The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties present in the flowers of Ocimum basilicum help reduce inflammation and fight infection in the gums.
Stems: The antimicrobial properties in the stems of Ocimum basilicum help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, promoting healthy gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ocimum basilicum leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated with clean scissors.
Chop 100g of the harvested leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife and mortar.
Combine the chopped leaves with 250ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The bioactive compounds present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to exhibit exceptional antibacterial activity against a wide range of microorganisms, including the bacteria that commonly cause gum infections like periodontitis. By applying thyme tincture directly to the affected area, it can help reduce inflammation, eliminate bacterial biofilms, and promote healthy gum tissue growth, ultimately supporting the body's natural defense against infection and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as carvacrol and thymol, help to combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause gum infections by disrupting their cell membranes and preventing them from reproducing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenolic compounds in thyme tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with gum infections, as well as alleviate discomfort and promote healing.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to the progression of gum infections, helping to protect the gums from oxidative stress and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to combat the bacterial growth that causes gum infections.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol has potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and eliminate the underlying cause of gum infections.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the gums, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial properties to help combat gum infections.
Barks: Utilized for their antiseptic and astringent properties to reduce inflammation and promote healing in gum infections.
Stems: Employed for their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties to prevent the spread of infection and promote gum health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers when in full bloom and dry them for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind the dried thymus vulgaris into a fine powder with a 1:5 ratio.
Combine the ground thymus vulgaris with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar with a 1:5 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and let it steep for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids, transferring the tincture to a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The tannins and phenolic acids in sage work together to reduce inflammation and eliminate harmful microorganisms from the mouth. This natural approach can help alleviate symptoms such as swelling, pain, and bad breath associated with gum infections, promoting a healthy oral environment and preventing further damage to teeth and surrounding tissues.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage tincture has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacterial species, including those associated with gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis.

remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid compound that helps to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections in the gums, thereby alleviating symptoms of gum infection.

Salvianolic acid B: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that helps to protect the gums from oxidative stress and reduces inflammation, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Carvacrol: A phenolic terpene with antimicrobial properties that helps to kill bacteria and other microorganisms responsible for gum infections, thereby reducing the severity and duration of the infection.

Stems: Contain compounds like rosmarinic acid, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, aiding in gum infection treatment.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh leaves from mature plants in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by spreading them on paper towels for 2-3 hours.
Combine the dried leaves with 80-proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it every day for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids before transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacteria and fungi that cause inflammation and discomfort. The tincture's bioactive compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxerol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful microorganisms, reducing swelling and alleviating pain in affected areas. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and sensitivity, promoting a healthy and balanced oral environment that allows gums to heal naturally.

Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial growth and prevent the spread of gum infections.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacteria and inflammation associated with gum infections.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the early morning to minimize moisture content.
Chop the harvested taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine 1 part chopped taraxacum officinale with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle to produce the herbal tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with gum infection because its active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, exhibit potent antimicrobial properties. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum disease, including Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis. Additionally, maidenhair tree tinctures contain anti-inflammatory agents that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum infection. By using these tinctures, individuals may experience relief from symptoms and promote a healthier oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Ginkgo biloba's antimicrobial compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help combat bacterial and fungal growth, reducing the severity of gum infections.

Inflammation reduction: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in Maidenhair Tree tincture, including flavoglycosides and bilobalide, decrease inflammation and swelling associated with gum disease, promoting healthy gum tissue.

Wound healing and tissue repair: Ginkgo biloba's saponins and other bioactive compounds aid in the regeneration of damaged gum tissue, accelerating the healing process and reducing the risk of chronic infections.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps with gum infection by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which reduces inflammation and promotes healing in the gums.

Ginkgolide B: Contributes to the treatment of gum infection by blocking platelet-activating factor (PAF), a chemical mediator involved in the inflammatory process, thereby reducing inflammation and preventing tissue damage.
Quercetin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums, promoting a faster recovery from gum infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are widely used in tinctures due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for gum infections.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are also used in tinctures to leverage their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate gum inflammation and promote healing.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are sometimes used in tinctures for their reported antimicrobial properties, which can aid in fighting off the bacterial or fungal infections causing gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 ginkgo biloba leaves and stems when they are fresh and not dried.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80-100 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid from the jar and discard the solids after 2-6 weeks of steeping time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric tinctures helps with gum infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Turmeric contains curcumin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of gum disease such as swelling, redness, and pain. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of turmeric can help combat the bacteria that cause gum infections, promoting healthy gums and reducing the risk of further infection. Regular use of tree turmeric tinctures may also help to reduce bleeding and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound Curcumin reduces inflammation, which helps alleviate gum swelling, pain, and redness associated with gum infections.

Antimicrobial: The bioactive compounds in Turmeric exhibit antimicrobial properties, effectively combating bacterial growth and promoting a healthy oral environment to prevent the spread of infection.
Immune-boosting: Turmeric's immunomodulatory effects help stimulate the immune system, enhancing its natural defense mechanisms against invading pathogens and accelerating the healing process.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome of Turmeric contains a high concentration of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of berberis aristata and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop 100g of the cleaned roots into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with gum infection because of its natural antimicrobial properties, which allow it to target and eliminate the bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The herb's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of a wide range of microorganisms, including those responsible for gum infections. As a result, St John's wort tinctures can help reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms, and promote healing in the gums, making it a valuable natural remedy for oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds: Jaborandi, a compound found in St John's Wort, has been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties, helping to combat the bacteria that cause gum infections, while also reducing inflammation and pain.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: The tannins and flavonoids present in St John's Wort have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with gum disease, promoting a healthier oral environment.

Hyperforin: This compound has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties against various types of bacteria, including those responsible for periodontal diseases, making it effective in combatting gum infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum infections.
Flowers: Contain hyperforin, a natural antibacterial agent that effectively targets bacteria causing gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers at dawn or early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, well-ventilated area or use a food dehydrator on low setting for 8 hours.
Grind 100g of dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 500ml of high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar, allowing it to infuse for 14 days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with gum infection because it possesses anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for combating gum infections. Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidant compounds help protect the gum tissue from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue regeneration and reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria, making them effective in treating gum infections.
Flowers: Lavender flowers have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe gum inflammation and prevent bacterial growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated and store them in airtight containers.
Combine 100 grams of harvested lavender flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka and let it infuse for 4 weeks shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and label the tincture as "lavender tincture".
Mix one teaspoon of the lavender tincture with an equal amount of water and swish it in your mouth for 30 seconds twice daily as a mouthwash.
Store the remaining tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in the refrigerator for up to two years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of origanum vulgare and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces using scissors or a knife to increase surface area exposure.
Combine one part chopped origanum vulgare leaves with five parts 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the jar at room temperature for two weeks, shaking daily to extract tannins and essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar discarding solids and preserve.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cuminum cyminum leaves and stems for maximum bioavailability.
Dry the harvested cuminum cyminum plant material in a warm ventilated area within 24 hours.
Grind the dried cuminum cyminum plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar pestle combination.
Mix 1 part ground cuminum cyminum with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar and allow to macerate for 6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids then store the tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tinctures helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The tincture's active compounds, such as carvone and limonene, have been shown to inhibit the proliferation of bacteria that adhere to tooth surfaces, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, caraway tinctures' antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate gum inflammation, making it a natural and effective remedy for combating gum infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Caraway tincture's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and limonene, inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in caraway tincture, including sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, allowing for improved wound healing and reduced discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Caraway tincture's antioxidant activity helps protect the mouth and gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum disease and infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the caraway plant have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating gum infections by reducing inflammation and combating bacteria.
Seeds: Caraway seeds contain compounds like thymol and carvone that have antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the severity of gum infections and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh carum carvi seeds on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested seeds in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7 days to prevent spoilage.
Combine 1 cup of dried carum carvi seeds with 4 cups of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and labeling date and contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with gum infection because it has natural antibacterial properties that combat bacteria responsible for causing periodontal disease. The tannins present in fennel help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, while its antiseptic compounds promote a healthy environment for healing. Additionally, fennel's ability to stimulate saliva production helps to neutralize acids and remineralize teeth, further supporting oral health. As a result, herbal fennel tinctures can provide effective relief from gum infection symptoms and support overall dental well-being.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Fennel's antimicrobial compounds, such as volatile oils and flavonoids, help combat bacteria and fungi that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, making it easier to manage symptoms and prevent further complications.
Antioxidant activity: Fennel's antioxidants help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum disease and infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and combating bacteria that cause gum infections.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain compounds like anethole and limonene, which exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities, helping to prevent the growth of pathogens that can cause gum infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried foeniculum vulgare seeds in sufficient quantity for the desired yield.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 60% to 70% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part herb to three parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing for proper maceration and extraction.

Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid tincture at a strength of 1:3 to 2:3 herbal material to solvent ratio.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in kava, such as kavalactones, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal diseases. Additionally, kava's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. As a natural antibacterial agent, kava tincture can be used topically or taken orally to combat gum infection, promoting healthy gums and preventing further damage. Its effectiveness has been backed by traditional use and modern research.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Kava's flavonoids and lactones have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the gums, helping to alleviate discomfort associated with gum infections.

Antibacterial activity: Kava's alkaloids, particularly kavalactones, exhibit antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial infections causing gum disease, such as gingivitis and periodontitis.

Analgesic and sedative effects: The same flavonoids and lactones responsible for anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties also have analgesic and sedative properties, which can help reduce pain, anxiety, and stress associated with gum infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the kava plant are rich in compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and killing bacteria responsible for gum infections.

Rhyzomes: Kava's rhyzomes contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial adhesion, making them useful in treating gum infection-related pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh kava root and chop it into small pieces.
Combine one part chopped root with three parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for two weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and label accordingly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers tinctures helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains triterpenoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Actinomyces naeslundii. Additionally, cleavers' antiseptic qualities help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, promoting a healthy healing environment. As a result, cleavers tinctures may be effective in reducing symptoms of gum infection, including bleeding, pain, and redness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cleavers tincture helps reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, promoting a healthy environment for healing.

Antimicrobial properties: The tincture's antimicrobial compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, preventing further infection and promoting recovery.
Immunomodulatory effects: Cleavers tincture has been shown to modulate the immune response, enhancing the body's natural defense against infection and promoting a balanced response to inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating gum infections by reducing swelling and preventing bacterial growth.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have antiseptic and astringent properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of infection in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has dried off.
Wash the harvested material gently with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to release essential oils.
Combine 250g of the chopped plant material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 14 days shaking the jar daily to allow extraction.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for gum infection are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms and prevent complications associated with gum disease. These tinctures work by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and combating bacteria that cause infection. Some of the best herbal tinctures for gum infection include Echinacea purpurea, Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Calendula officinalis, Hydrastis canadensis, Salvia officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Sanguinaria canadensis, Zingiber officinale, and Commiphora molmol. If left untreated, gum infection can lead to pain, tooth loss, and systemic infections, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions that affect mucous membranes and soft tissues. For example, thrush is a fungal infection of the mouth and throat that can be treated with tea tree oil or myrrh tincture. Additionally, genital herpes and other sexually transmitted infections (STIs) can be soothed by using herbal tinctures such as lemon balm or calendula.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with gum infection is a blend of Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Myrrh. Echinacea enhances the immune system, while Goldenseal's berberine reduces inflammation and fights bacteria. Myrrh's antimicrobial properties help combat the infection, promoting healing and reducing pain. This synergistic blend can be taken orally or applied topically to the affected area, providing a natural and effective solution to treat and prevent gum infection. Consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Ayu, herbal tinctures, specifically those made from Ocimum sanctum (Tulsi) and Aloe vera, can be beneficial for gum infection due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The study, which involved 60 patients randomly allocated into three groups (Ocimum sanctum mouthwash, Aloe vera mouthwash, and chlorhexidine mouthwash), found that these herbal mouthwashes were equally effective as chlorhexidine in reducing plaque, gingival, and bleeding indices after 30 days. This suggests that herbal tinctures made from these plants can be a viable alternative to chlorhexidine in the long-term treatment of gum infections.

The tannins and flavonoids present in the herb exhibit potent antibacterial action, inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis. The tincture, made from the dried roots of the plant, contains compounds like benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to gum infections. Commiphora molmol tinctures helps with gum infection because it contains antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria and fungi causing the infection. The tannins and essential oils present in the tincture reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gum tissue.
